name: Henry Cejudo

qualityWinLedger.context: All-time flyweight champion; close decision flagged but max opponent quality.
Elite bantamweight contender for vacant title.
UFC bantamweight champion moving down with weight-cut context. Approved division/timing calibration.
All-time bantamweight name with long-layoff timing. Approved division/timing calibration.
Former title challenger but limited timing value.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: DJ II starts prime. Retirement gap capped. Sterling comeback title loss closes it; Merab is post-prime/comeback context.

shortCase: Cejudo is one of the best short-window legacy cases in the sport: flyweight champion, bantamweight champion, and a fighter who packed a lot of value into a compact UFC run.
bestArgument: Cejudo wins when compact championship achievement and two-division value matter more than long-term title volume.
counterArgument: Cejudo’s argument is peak efficiency. He did a lot in a small window, and his best wins are loud enough to make him dangerous in almost any comparison.
whyRankedHere: Cejudo ranks here because he packed major value into a short UFC window: flyweight title, bantamweight title, the Demetrious Johnson win, and a fast run through elite names.
whyNotHigher: He does not rank higher because the title window is short and the total UFC volume is limited. The achievements are loud, but the long-reign proof is not there.
finalTakeaway: Cejudo is the compact double-champ case: one of the most efficient achievement bursts in UFC history, but too short to outrank deeper long-reign resumes.
